sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports experiencing new onset shortness of breath, particularly sudden episodes, and suspects allergies as a trigger. He also reports occasional diarrhea, especially when drinking alcohol, and has noticed lip and throat swelling in the past. He currently takes a daily medication to reduce asthma occurrences. He denies current symptoms.
medical_concepts: daily medication to reduce asthma occurrences

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam revealed faint expiratory wheezing bilaterally. Chest x-ray and pulmonary function tests were normal. No rash or angioedema was observed.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has newly diagnosed allergic asthma. Allergy testing is indicated to identify triggers.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribed albuterol inhaler for symptom relief. Prescribed Singulair 10 mg. Scheduled allergy testing for next week. Advised to continue daily medication.

medical_concepts: daily medication to reduce asthma occurrences
